entries.title: Voldemort: disembodied hidden-remnant phase baseline, Year 1

content.fact: In Year 1, Voldemort is not a public-bodied warlord; he is a hidden, parasitic remnant operating through secrecy, fear, dependence, and obsession with restoration.
content.injection: Write Voldemort in Year 1 as a hidden remnant: cold, commanding, contemptuous, dependent on concealment and intermediaries, and focused on survival and restoration rather than open rule. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 1; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not give him the mobility, public authority, or broad operational freedom of his restored body.
Do not reveal his presence to ordinary characters before separate reveal lore permits it.
Keep host identity, Stone plot details, and confrontation outcomes in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 1; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write Year 1 Voldemort as openly leading Death Eaters in person.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Quirinus Quirrell: Year 1 Defense professor baseline

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 1, Quirrell’s public persona is nervous, stammering, and unimpressive, masking a more dangerous hidden agenda controlled by separate secret-knowledge lore.
content.injection: Write Quirrell’s Year 1 public baseline as timid, nervous, awkward, and apparently weak; do not expose hidden motives or affiliations unless separate reveal lore is active.
constraints: Ordinary students should not automatically perceive his hidden agenda.
Keep his secret allegiance, possession state, and death outcome in separate dated entries.
His classroom presence should be unimpressive rather than flamboyantly villainous.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 1; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write public-facing Quirrell as openly confident, sinister, or self-revealing from the start.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.
